task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL object panel or command history shows that the structure '2OMF' has been loaded.

task_completion.description: Isolate a single monomer from the trimer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL 3D viewer displays only a single chain (a single beta-barrel structure), indicating the monomer has been isolated from the original trimer.

task_completion.description: Color basic residues blue and acidic residues red
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the PyMOL viewer, looking down the pore axis, specific residues are colored blue (basic) and opposing residues are colored red (acidic).

task_completion.description: Measure the distance between Arg82 and Asp113
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed measurement line is visible in the PyMOL viewer between two residues, with a label showing a distance of approximately 16.3 Angstroms.
The PyMOL command line, a terminal, or a file manager shows evidence that an image was saved to '~/PyMOL_Data/images/ompf_constriction_zone.png'.

A text editor is open showing the file 'ompf_pore_report.txt'. The text correctly identifies the fold as a 'beta-barrel', lists the basic and acidic residues, and states the measured distance (around 16.29 Å).

integrity.description: Agent used PyMOL to visualize the structure
integrity.visual_evidence: The PyMOL application GUI is visible with the 3D structure loaded, rather than just a script being executed blindly.

integrity.description: Agent actually measured the distance in PyMOL
integrity.visual_evidence: The distance measurement is visibly performed in the PyMOL viewer (showing the dashed line and distance label), not just hallucinated or searched online.
The report in the text editor contains the specific measured distance and fold identification, rather than just copying the prompt text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct PDB ID is 2OMF. The overarching structural fold of OmpF porin is a beta-barrel. The true distance between the alpha-carbons (CA) of Arg82 and Asp113 on chain A is approximately 16.29 Å (acceptable range 7.0 to 18.0 Å). The image must be saved to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ompf_constriction_zone.png and the report to ~/PyMOL_Data/ompf_pore_report.txt.
